notes: All 21 steps completed (1 prerequisite + 20 TDD steps). Implemented autopilot/render/validate.py with validate_render(), ValidationReport, Issue, ValidationError. Six quality checks: duration, resolution/codec, file size, loudness, black frames, silence detection. All 45 tests pass. Updated render/__init__.py exports.

summary: Create empty autopilot/render/validate.py with module docstring and __all__; Write failing tests for public API surface: ValidationReport dataclass (passed, issues list with severity/message/measurement, measurements dict), ValidationError exception, validate_render function signature (rendered_path: Path, edl: dict, config: OutputConfig) -> ValidationReport, and __all__ exports; Implement ValidationReport dataclass with Issue(severity, check, message, measured_value) inner dataclass, ValidationError exception class, validate_render stub returning empty ValidationReport, and __all__ exports. Update render/__init__.py to export new symbols.; Write failing tests for _run_ffprobe_json helper: mock subprocess.run to return ffprobe JSON with streams and format info; verify it extracts duration, resolution, codec, file size; test error handling when ffprobe fails (returns empty dict or raises); Implement _run_ffprobe_json(path) that calls ffprobe -v quiet -print_format json -show_format -show_streams, parses stdout JSON, returns dict with duration_seconds, resolution (w,h), video_codec, audio_codec, file_size_bytes. Handle subprocess errors gracefully.; Write failing tests for duration check: mock ffprobe to return duration. Test pass when within ±1s of EDL target_duration_seconds; test error when duration off by >1s; test warning/skip when edl has no target_duration_seconds; Implement _check_duration(probe_data, edl, issues) comparing probe duration to edl['target_duration_seconds'] within ±1s tolerance, appending Issue with severity='error' when out of range. Wire into validate_render.; Write failing tests for resolution and codec check: test pass when probe resolution matches config.resolution and codec matches config.codec; test error on resolution mismatch; test error on codec mismatch; Implement _check_resolution_codec(probe_data, config, issues) comparing probe resolution/codec against OutputConfig values, appending error Issues on mismatch. Wire into validate_render.; Write failing tests for file size check: compute expected range as 8-15 MB per minute for 1080p h264. Test pass when file size within range; test warning when below 8 MB/min; test warning when above 15 MB/min; test that non-1080p or non-h264 skips the check; Implement _check_file_size(probe_data, config, issues) computing per-minute bitrate and comparing to 8-15 MB/min threshold for 1080p h264, appending warning Issues when outside range. Wire into validate_render.; Write failing tests for loudness check: mock subprocess.run for ffmpeg loudnorm analysis pass (ffmpeg -i file -af loudnorm=print_format=json -f null -). Parse measured LUFS from stderr JSON. Test pass when within -16±1 LUFS; test error when outside range; test error when ffmpeg fails; Implement _check_loudness(rendered_path, config, issues) running ffmpeg loudnorm analysis pass, parsing measured integrated loudness from stderr JSON output, appending error Issue when outside target_loudness_lufs ± 1 LUFS. Wire into validate_render.; Write failing tests for black frame detection: mock subprocess.run for ffmpeg blackdetect filter. Test pass when no black frames detected; test warning when black frames found (stderr contains blackdetect output); test graceful handling when ffmpeg fails; Implement _check_black_frames(rendered_path, issues) running ffmpeg -i file -vf blackdetect=d=0.1:pix_th=5 -f null -, parsing blackdetect output from stderr for black_start/black_end/black_duration fields, appending warning Issues for each detected range. Wire into validate_render.; Write failing tests for silence detection: mock subprocess.run for ffmpeg silencedetect filter. Test pass when no silence >2s detected; test warning when silence >2s found; test that intentional silence marked in EDL is excluded; test graceful handling when ffmpeg fails; Implement _check_silence(rendered_path, edl, issues) running ffmpeg -i file -af silencedetect=n=-50dB:d=2 -f null -, parsing silence_start/silence_end/silence_duration from stderr, filtering out EDL-marked intentional silences, appending warning Issues. Wire into validate_render.; Write failing tests for validate_render end-to-end: mock all subprocess calls. Test full pass scenario returns passed=True with measurements dict populated. Test mixed issues scenario returns passed=False with correct error/warning counts. Test report JSON serialization.; Complete validate_render orchestration: call ffprobe once, run all checks, populate measurements dict (duration, loudness_lufs, file_size_mb, resolution, codec), set passed=True only if no error-severity issues, return ValidationReport. Add to_dict() method for JSON serialization.; Write failing tests for validation_report.json writing: validate_render should write validation_report.json to output/{narrative_title}/ directory. Test file is created with correct content. Test handles missing output directory by creating it.; Add report writing to validate_render: derive output directory from rendered_path parent, write validation_report.json with JSON-serialized ValidationReport. Create directory if needed.
Debug fix for: Failures: lint issues
Completed all 6 remaining robustness steps (21-26). Added ValueError guards for _check_black_frames and _check_silence float() parsing, and OSError guard for report file writing. All 51 tests pass.

Completed all 20 steps + 1 prerequisite for YouTube upload and thumbnail task. Created autopilot/upload/youtube.py (UploadError, upload_video with OAuth creds, metadata assembly, resumable upload, retry), autopilot/upload/thumbnail.py (ThumbnailError, extract_best_thumbnail with Laplacian sharpness, rule-of-thirds, detection confidence scoring, frame extraction, optional YouTube thumbnail upload), scripts/setup_youtube_oauth.py (OAuth setup script). Updated upload/__init__.py exports. All 35 tests pass.
